# FAKE example secrets for testing repo-airlock detectors.
# None of these are real credentials. AWS example key below is the
# well-known public AWS documentation example key.
#
# NOTE: Slack (xoxb-), Slack webhook, Stripe (sk_live_/sk_test_), and Google
# (AIza...) tokens are deliberately NOT present as literals in this data file —
# GitHub's secret scanner rejects/alerts on those realistic formats even when
# fake. Those detectors are covered in tests/test_secrets_detector.py, where the
# token values are assembled at runtime from fragments so no contiguous literal
# is committed.

aws_access_key_id = AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE
aws_secret_access_key = wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY

github_token = ghp_1234567890abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz12
github_fine_grained = github_pat_11ABCDEFG0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz01234567890ABCDEFGHIJKLMN

fake_jwt = eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIiwibmFtZSI6IkpvaG4gRG9lIn0.dozjgNryP4J3jVmNHl0w5N_XgL0n3I9PYlM3nQwcHQE

password = "hunter2superSecretValue"

-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
MIIEpAIBAAKCAQEAfakefakefakefakefakefakefakefakefakefakefakefake
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
